agent_output: {"domain_analysis":{"business_context":"Parcel delivery logistics platform managing shipment lifecycle from creation through transit to delivery, with weight-based pricing tiers.","bounded_context":"Shipment Management — owns shipment lifecycle (creation, state transitions, delivery finalization) and weight-based classification for billing.","ubiquitous_language":[{"term":"Shipment","definition":"A tracked parcel moving from a pickup address to a delivery address, progressing through defined lifecycle states."},{"term":"Pickup Address","definition":"The origin location where a parcel is collected from the sender."},{"term":"Delivery Address","definition":"The destination location where a parcel is delivered to the recipient."},{"term":"Pending","definition":"Initial shipment state after creation, before transit begins."},{"term":"In Transit","definition":"Shipment state indicating the parcel is actively being transported. Irreversible — cannot return to Pending."},{"term":"Delivered","definition":"Terminal shipment state indicating successful delivery. No further state changes permitted."},{"term":"Weight Bracket","definition":"Classification of a shipment by its weight, determining the pricing tier applied."},{"term":"Standard","definition":"Weight bracket for shipments under 10 kg."},{"term":"Heavy","definition":"Weight bracket for shipments from 10 kg (inclusive) up to but not including 50 kg."},{"term":"Freight","definition":"Weight bracket for shipments of 50 kg (inclusive) and above, up to the 1000 kg maximum."}]},"business_rules":[{"id":"BR-1","rule":"A shipment can only be created when both a pickup address and a delivery address are provided.","invariant":true,"invariant_justification":"A shipment without both endpoints is logically meaningless — it cannot be routed or delivered. This is a data integrity constraint.","testable_assertion":"Any attempt to create a shipment missing either pickup address or delivery address must be rejected."},{"id":"BR-2","rule":"Shipment state transitions follow the sequence: Pending → In Transit → Delivered. No other transitions are permitted.","invariant":true,"invariant_justification":"State integrity constraint. Allowing backward or skip transitions would corrupt shipment tracking and operational coordination.","testable_assertion":"A shipment in 'In Transit' state cannot transition to 'Pending'. A shipment in 'Delivered' state cannot transition to any other state."},{"id":"BR-3","rule":"Delivered is a terminal state — no further state changes are allowed on a delivered shipment.","invariant":true,"invariant_justification":"Finality of delivery is a core logistics invariant. Reopening delivered shipments would compromise audit trails and billing records.","testable_assertion":"Any attempt to change the state of a Delivered shipment must be rejected regardless of the target state."},{"id":"BR-4","rule":"Shipment weight must be strictly positive (greater than zero).","invariant":true,"invariant_justification":"A parcel with zero or negative weight is physically impossible and would corrupt billing calculations.","testable_assertion":"Any shipment with weight <= 0 must be rejected at creation."},{"id":"BR-5","rule":"Shipment weight must not exceed 1000 kg.","invariant":false,"invariant_justification":"The 1000 kg cap is a business policy reflecting current vehicle/infrastructure limits. A VP could raise this threshold if fleet capacity changes.","testable_assertion":"Any shipment with weight > 1000 kg must be rejected."},{"id":"BR-6","rule":"Weight bracket 'Standard' applies to shipments with weight strictly less than 10 kg.","invariant":false,"invariant_justification":"Bracket boundaries are pricing policy, adjustable by business decision.","testable_assertion":"A shipment weighing 9.99 kg is classified as Standard. A shipment weighing 10 kg is NOT Standard."},{"id":"BR-7","rule":"Weight bracket 'Heavy' applies to shipments with weight >= 10 kg and < 50 kg.","invariant":false,"invariant_justification":"Bracket boundaries are pricing policy, adjustable by business decision.","testable_assertion":"A shipment weighing exactly 10 kg is classified as Heavy. A shipment weighing 49.99 kg is classified as Heavy. A shipment weighing 50 kg is NOT Heavy."},{"id":"BR-8","rule":"Weight bracket 'Freight' applies to shipments with weight >= 50 kg (up to the 1000 kg maximum).","invariant":false,"invariant_justification":"Bracket boundaries are pricing policy, adjustable by business decision.","testable_assertion":"A shipment weighing exactly 50 kg is classified as Freight. A shipment weighing 1000 kg is classified as Freight."}],"acceptance_criteria":[{"id":"AC-1","given":"A request to create a shipment with both a pickup address and a delivery address provided","when":"The shipment is created","then":"The shipment is persisted in Pending state with the provided addresses."},{"id":"AC-2","given":"A request to create a shipment where the pickup address is missing","when":"The creation is attempted","then":"The system rejects the request and no shipment is created."},{"id":"AC-3","given":"A request to create a shipment where the delivery address is missing","when":"The creation is attempted","then":"The system rejects the request and no shipment is created."},{"id":"AC-4","given":"A shipment in Pending state","when":"It is moved to In Transit","then":"The shipment state becomes In Transit."},{"id":"AC-5","given":"A shipment in In Transit state","when":"An attempt is made to move it back to Pending","then":"The system rejects the transition and the shipment remains In Transit."},{"id":"AC-6","given":"A shipment in In Transit state","when":"It is moved to Delivered","then":"The shipment state becomes Delivered."},{"id":"AC-7","given":"A shipment in Delivered state","when":"An attempt is made to change its state to any other state","then":"The system rejects the transition and the shipment remains Delivered."},{"id":"AC-8","given":"A shipment with weight 0 or negative","when":"Creation is attempted","then":"The system rejects the request."},{"id":"AC-9","given":"A shipment with weight exceeding 1000 kg","when":"Creation is attempted","then":"The system rejects the request."},{"id":"AC-10","given":"A shipment with weight 5 kg","when":"The weight bracket is determined","then":"The shipment is classified as Standard."},{"id":"AC-11","given":"A shipment with weight exactly 10 kg","when":"The weight bracket is determined","then":"The shipment is classified as Heavy."},{"id":"AC-12","given":"A shipment with weight 49.99 kg","when":"The weight bracket is determined","then":"The shipment is classified as Heavy."},{"id":"AC-13","given":"A shipment with weight exactly 50 kg","when":"The weight bracket is determined","then":"The shipment is classified as Freight."},{"id":"AC-14","given":"A shipment with weight exactly 1000 kg","when":"The weight bracket is determined","then":"The shipment is classified as Freight."},{"id":"AC-15","given":"A shipment in Pending state","when":"An attempt is made to move it directly to Delivered","then":"The system rejects the transition (must go through In Transit first)."}],"edge_cases":[{"scenario":"Weight at exact bracket boundary of 10 kg","expected_behavior":"Classified as Heavy (10 kg is the inclusive lower boundary of the Heavy bracket)."},{"scenario":"Weight at exact bracket boundary of 50 kg","expected_behavior":"Classified as Freight (50 kg is the inclusive lower boundary of the Freight bracket)."},{"scenario":"Weight at the maximum of exactly 1000 kg","expected_behavior":"Accepted and classified as Freight."},{"scenario":"Weight of 1000.01 kg","expected_behavior":"Rejected — exceeds the 1000 kg maximum."},{"scenario":"Weight that is extremely small but positive (e.g., 0.001 kg)","expected_behavior":"Accepted and classified as Standard — weight is positive and under 10 kg."},{"scenario":"Attempt to skip Pending and create shipment directly in In Transit or Delivered","expected_behavior":"Rejected — all shipments must start in Pending state."},{"scenario":"Attempt to transition Pending directly to Delivered, skipping In Transit","expected_behavior":"Rejected — the only valid forward transition from Pending is to In Transit."}],"business_impact":{"financial":"Weight bracket classification directly drives pricing and revenue. Incorrect bracket assignment means incorrect billing — either revenue leakage (undercharging) or customer disputes (overcharging).","operational":"Enforced state machine prevents ghost shipments and audit inconsistencies. The 1000 kg cap protects against logistics planning failures where infrastructure cannot handle oversized loads.","user":"Clear rejection messages on invalid creation or transitions prevent user confusion. Correct bracket assignment builds trust in invoicing accuracy.","risk":"Low overall risk — the rules are explicit and well-bounded. Primary risk is at bracket boundaries where off-by-one errors in implementation could misclassify shipments at 10 kg or 50 kg exactly.","stakeholders_affected":[{"group":"Shippers (customers)","impact":"Billing accuracy depends on correct weight bracket classification. State visibility depends on correct lifecycle enforcement."},{"group":"Operations / Dispatch","impact":"State machine integrity ensures reliable operational workflows — dispatchers can trust that In Transit means actually in transit."},{"group":"Finance / Billing","impact":"Revenue recognition depends on correct bracket assignment and shipment finality (Delivered is immutable for invoicing)."},{"group":"Warehouse Staff","impact":"Weight validation at creation prevents impossible-to-handle parcels from entering the system."}]},"confidence":{"level":95,"reasoning":"The specification is explicit, internally consistent, and covers all necessary dimensions: lifecycle states, transition rules, weight constraints, and bracket boundaries with clear inclusive/exclusive boundary definitions. No contradictions, no ambiguity, no missing context.","high_confidence_areas":["State machine transitions are unambiguous — Pending to In Transit to Delivered, no reversals, Delivered is terminal","Weight brackets have explicitly stated inclusive/exclusive boundaries","Weight constraints (positive, max 1000 kg) are clear","Creation prerequisites (both addresses required) are clear"],"low_confidence_areas":["Whether Pending-to-Delivered skip is allowed is not explicitly stated but strongly implied by the linear progression described — treated as disallowed","Whether weight can be changed after shipment creation is not addressed — assumed immutable once created"],"assumptions":["Shipments always start in Pending state upon creation","Pending cannot skip directly to Delivered — must pass through In Transit","Weight is set at creation time and does not change during the shipment lifecycle","Addresses are non-empty strings — the spec says they must be provided but does not define address validation rules"]},"escalations":[],"rejection_reasons":[]}
agent_output_excerpt: {"domain_analysis":{"business_context":"Parcel delivery logistics platform managing shipment lifecycle from creation through transit to delivery, with weight-based pricing tiers.","bounded_context":"Shipment Management — owns shipment lifecycle (creation, state transitions, delivery finalization) and weight-based classification for billing.","ubiquitous_language":[{"term":"Shipment","definition":"A tracked parcel moving from a pickup address to a delivery address, progressing through defined lif

content.thinking:
Let me analyze this logistics domain scenario carefully.

The rules are clear enough. Let me parse them:

1. Shipment creation requires pickup and delivery address
2. State transitions: pending -> in_transit -> delivered (no back to pending from in_transit, delivered is final)
3. Weight: positive, max 1000kg
4. Weight brackets: <10kg standard, 10-49.99kg heavy, >=50kg freight

No contradictions, no ambiguity, no missing context. This is a straightforward spec.
